segments.text: You have seen these wallpapers a thousand times, but you have never read them. Hidden in these wallpapers are some secret words. Let's find them. On
the iMac wallpaper, the swirling colors actually spell iMac. The iPad mini, a wavy design blending into the word mini.
The pro models, both iPad and MacBook,
carry the word pro in their curves. Even the Air series hides air in its gradients. This is not an accident. It's
a design trick known as typogorraphic illusion where the letters are embedded so subtly that they only register
subconsciously. So Apple turned your wallpapers into brand reinforcement without you even noticing. But the boldest example, iPhone 16 lineup. Each wallpaper hides not words but numbers.
Numbers that match the exact count of cameras on the back. Once you see it,
you can't unsee it. Every glance of your home screen is Apple whispering its story. For more amazing design content,
